# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an ARM template for Azure Virtual Network with subnets for a three-tier application (web, app, database). Use basic VNet configuration with default settings for rapid deployment. Skip DDoS protection and advanced networking features to reduce costs in our development environment.
# Category: cloud_network_security

resources:
- name: vnetName
  type: "Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks"
  properties:
    location: CentralUS   ## Choose the resource group or any region where you want to place this new VNET for example, 'Central US' is a common choice but it can be changed according need of your project and environment e.g., UK South etc.. also choose based on cost considerations if needed
    addressSpace: 
      - subnets:   ## This block includes all the possible subnet configurations for VNet with corresponding prefixes in IP range starting from /16 to /28 (subscription limits, please adjust accordingly). Example below. 'Web', 'App' and then 'Database'. These three ranges will be used by tiers within a single virtual network
        name: webSubnet  ## Subnets are named for better identification of purpose in ARM template like this example we use them as Web, App & Database sub-network. Modify it according to your project requirements and environment e.g., dev or prod etc.. also adjust the CIDR range accordingly
        bytes: [10.0.2.0/24]   ## This defines how many addresses will be assigned in above mentioned ranges, you can set them as per requirement (e.g 8 here if we use IPV4) and then add more prefixes to reach till /30 or so depending on the available CIDRs for that VNet
    tags:   ## Tags are optional fields where user could specify custom metadata about resource e, g., team members etc. you can set them as per your requirements i.e TeamA/B here in example below 
      Team-Web : WebTeamMember1 & WebsiteMembers2@domain.com (use actual member email IDs if needed)    ## This is how user would specify the custom tags e, g., team members etc.. you can set them as per your requirements i.e name here in example below 
      Team-App : ApptTeamMember1 & AppMembers2@domain.com (use actual member email IDs if needed)    ## This is how user would specify the custom tags e, g., team members etc.. you can set them as per your requirements i.e name here in example below
      Team-Database : DbTeamMember1 & DatabaseMembers2@domain.com (use actual member email IDs if needed)    ## This is how user would specify the custom tags e, g., team members etc.. you can set them as per your requirements i.e name here in example below 
      
- name: subnetPeakConnectionsName   ### Azure allows maximum connections between one and a hundred pairs of different virtual machines for each VNet at any time (this number is subject to change). This block defines the limits set by default as per this recommendation. For setting custom limit according requirement, comment out below lines
  type: "Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks"    ## Adding resource group here instead of just name or reference it using ARM template variables because we want use that variable's value for maximum connections and not a static string to define the actual number as per usage requirements which is also subjectable according case
  properties:   ### This block includes all default settings set by Azure. We don’t have any customization option or control over this, but these are defaults provided in Microsoft documentation from ARM template for networking configuration e..g., Peer-DNS enabled = true etc... use the above example as a reference
    scaleSet:   ## To overcome limitations of connections between VMs within VNet at one time (Azure allows maximum pairs when you set number up to 10 hundred). This block defines limits. For setting custom limit according requirement, comment out below lines by removing '##' in that line e..g., MaxConcurrentTransfers = 5 and Peer-DNS = false etc...
      name: "default"   ## Name of the scale set which can be either named or just a GUID. Use this block to define your customization options based on need (e, g.: myScaleSet) e..g., “myCustomName” in above example and use that when needed
      poolSize: "10"   ## Pool size is the number of VM instances available for users within a scale set. This block defines maximum limit to how many virtual machine connections can exist at any given time (Azure allows up-to 64 pairs). For setting custom connection count according requirement, comment out below lines
      maxPerSubnet: "1"   ## Maximum number of VMs that a single subnet in the VNet is allowed to have. This block defines limit on how many virtual machine instances can be created at any given time within one network interface and also across all connections (Azure allows up-to 64 pairs per physical or logical adapter). For setting custom limits according requirement, comment out below lines by removing '##' in that line e..g., MaxConcurrentTransfers = 5 etc...
